question: What does the always-on doctrine chain actually cost per task — tokens, dollars, turns, wall time — full vs trimmed vs bare, on identical work?
hypothesis: Mostly cache-amortized: live telemetry shows 640-1050x cache-read-to-fresh-input ratios, so the marginal dollar cost of the ~15.8k-token chain should be small. If measured cost or latency deltas are large, criterion-6 (fast/low-latency) work should target the chain; if small, hook process overhead is the real latency surface, not doctrine weight.
